text:
There once was a girl named Mariko who collected mushrooms from the forest. One day she found a peculiar one. She placed it atop her hat, curious.

By nightfall, a owl landed beside her. It spoke: "You always say 'owl'd at stars?' Try one. You'd sing like an owl!"

Mariko crunched down its berries. Whew!

"The night is dark – what'll you do?" asked an owl.

She thought: "Just keep quiet and listen to the breeze through those leaves."

The owl sang a little tune.

"You dance like an owl!"

Mariko was humming along. 

That night, Mariko didn't go home alone – a wise old owl and a young rabbit joined her. They talked the whole night until dawn.

She says now, decades later: "You know, I always thought I'd sound like a duck…"<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
